description:
GOAL: Wire heap-allocated cons cells through the entire CPS pipeline. First heap type to actually run via JIT.

## Decisions captured

- List = HeapKind::List (NOT a Struct — special-cased for traversal hotness; head/tail loads are direct 8-byte reads at fixed offsets).
- Cons cell layout: HeapHeader (16) + head:FzValue (8) + tail:FzValue (8) = 32 bytes total.
- Builtins / IR prims (defined in .11.3 placeholder; populated here): cons / head / tail / is_nil. length is derived (or builtin).
- Codegen: cons calls fz_alloc_list_cons; head/tail are 8-byte loads at offset 16/24.

## Deliverables

- IR translation for List exprs in src/ir_lower.rs (already covered in .11.4 — this ticket connects to runtime).
- IR codegen for Prim::ListCons / ListHead / ListTail / ListIsNil.
- Builtins wiring (interp side reuses existing FzVec / List logic; JIT side uses heap cons cells).
- GC integration: cons cells found via header.kind == List; traced via list-special branch in .11.2 tracer.

## Tests

- `fn main() do print([1, 2, 3]) end` runs through JIT.
- `fn sum([]): 0; fn sum([h | t]): h + sum(t); main: print(sum([1,2,3,4,5]))` -> 15.
- `fn map(_, []): []; fn map(f, [h|t]): [f(h) | map(f, t)]` (heap-allocated higher-order list traversal).
- Allocate a list, drop reference, force GC, verify list freed.

## Out of scope

- Other heap types (.11.11-.11.14, separate tickets).

comments.text: Landing notes (2026-05-10): 3 of 4 specified tests landed (print-list, sum via head/tail, GC reclaim + GC keep counterpart). The 4th — `map(f, [h|t])` higher-order — deferred to fz-ul4.11.19 (Closures end-to-end via CPS), authorized at landing time. .11.19 blocked by .11.11 so closure schemas can ride on the general struct path.

title: Lists as first heap type, end-to-end via CPS
